You stumble against one of the plant beds, losing your balance and nearly falling into the greenery when a green arm grabs hold of you and pulls you back on your feet. “Careful there stranger,” the woman chuckles. “This bed is already full of enough pretty flowers without adding you to the mix.” Once she has you standing properly, the woman – clearly the shopkeeper based on her dirt-stained and flower-patterned apron – pats your legs clean, then your back and finally your head in a decidedly motherly gesture. “You alright, sweetie?” You can’t help but blush at her soft and kind mannerism. If she isn’t a mother, then your name isn’t Steele. Looking at her again, bending down to coo at the plants you nearly squashed like young children, you see that she isn’t some camouflaged dryad but [var0 |a thraggen |a member of a more typical, if imposing, humanoid race ]. Still, her green skin and bright red hair do make her fade into the background of plants and bright flowers surrounding you, so it’s no wonder you hadn’t spotted her before she moved. And [var0 |as a thraggen |whatever she is ], she is also quite tall and well-toned. As softly as she treated you or those plants, the muscles you see bulging out of her sleeves are anything but. Yet you can’t help but feel safe right now, and when she stands back up and [pc.heightRange 0 72 84 |towers over |faces |looks up at ] you, the warm and cheerful smile she gives you forces a similar one to sprout on your lips, [pc.kmhbb |if only to make her as happy as she made you |though you add a wink to make her laugh |despite your best efforts |and why shouldn’t you?! Like, everyone should smile like that all the time anyway |no harm in it anyway ]. “Sorry about scaring you like that, sweetie,” she apologizes, though her tone is quite merry. “Wouldn’t think a thraggen would be any good at sneaking, but you wouldn’t believe how often that happens. I’ve gotten quite good at catching people by now!” [pc.kmhbb |You chuckle with her and sincerely |Why, is she calling you a catch? Still, you do |She better be, if she doesn’t want to get sued. Still, you suppose you should |You are, like, a catch after all! But still you, like, |Not like someone like you would get hurt by such a small fall, but anyway, you ] apologize for nearly crushing her flowers. She simply waves your apology off. “Nonsense! Our lil’ greens aren’t going to be hurt by one cute tooshie landing on them. If anything, I’d like to take their place,” she says with a playful wink, giving your butt a soft pat. Right, well, good to know. But also, she said ‘our’ earlier? “Yes, of course. I don’t think I could take care of this place all on my own. But my husband Horkenn and I – name’s Orroah by the way – manage just fine! Speaking of-” You follow her gaze to look behind you, and you jump again when you see a second massive thraggen standing right beside you. Putting aside how you might not have seen Orroah earlier, how did you not hear this one coming at all? He’s built like a brick house, with a full foot of height over his already tall wife! And again you feel yourself falling, this time because you slipped on a patch of dirt as you turned around so quickly. And again, a powerful green arm catches you, this time holding on to your torso. The size of the hand grabbing you and the ease with which he lifts you up and puts you back down on your feet makes you feel like a doll he picked up. Though it’s clear by how slowly he went, that he took extreme care not to use any more strength than was strictly necessary. “Sorry,” the new thraggen, Horkenn, says as he pats you down much like Orroah did, though his pats are much slower and careful. What few muscles you can see out of his sleeves – wait, are they wearing matching outfits? Adorable. Anyway, what you can see aren’t the chiseled muscles of his wife but a much beefier look, covered in a layer of fat that hides the muscles’ definition but only adds to the body’s overall power. As you can attest by the fact that you still feel pushed around by such slow and careful pats. “Are you okay, [pc.boyGirl]?” Well, as expected of such a motherly woman’s husband, this man feels incredibly fatherly. If those two told you they didn’t have kids yet, you’d call them liars. At any rate, now standing between two massive, older and kind folks you suddenly feel like a kid again. You assure him you’re fine, in a timid voice that you last used when you were much younger. You remember your manners and thank him, before introducing yourself to them in turn. “And we’re glad to meet you too, [pc.name]!” Orroah exclaims happily, while Horkenn simply grunts and nods with a smile. “I hope you enjoyed our little paradise despite the surprises.” Oh, you very much did; the place is idyllic to say the least. And it only got better once the two of them came in. “Oh you little charmer, you. But let’s not do this here. Would you like to come in and have a cup of tea with us?” Orroah offers. Do they offer tea to all of their customers? Not that you’d say no to such a kind offer. “Customer?” they both ask in synchronized confusion. “Oh right,” the motherly thraggen adds with a laugh. “We are a flower shop, aren’t we?” “We are?” the fatherly thraggen says, still sounding awfully confused. “I thought you said we worked for the plants, now.” “Oh honey, that was a joke. Well, mostly. Now come on, then.” She turns to walk through the narrow gaps between flower beds, but you hesitate at first since it’s almost a struggle to see the floor below the errant leaves blocking your way. You jerk with a start when a large hand grabs your own, and you find yourself gently led through the shop by Horkenn. [pc.heightRange 0 72 96 |The big man even bends down almost double to make it easier on you, which results in him getting a faceful of leaves at every turn |He has to walk sideways while keeping hold of your hand to avoid falling |He clearly doesn’t seem used to holding the hand of someone taller than he is, so his confusion leads him to occasionally get facefuls of leaves ]. He doesn’t let it bother him though, and eventually you arrive at your destination. Compared to the rest of the shop, where plants fill nearly every available space, this is like a small clearing in the jungle. The flower beds stop to make way for a round wooden table, surrounded by chairs of a similar design. You’re still surrounded by walls of leaves and flowers, but in this little circle here you can at least stretch your arms without hitting anything. And you notice this table is close to one of the walls, set near a door that must lead to their living quarters, judging by how homely this place looks. You almost look up expecting to see the roof of a gazebo. It’s very nice, and you say as much. “This little peaceful nook is our own private haven,” Orroah tells you proudly, clearly happy to see you like it. “More than that, it’s what saved us from ourselves.” She presents a seat for you and motions for you to sit, which you do while wondering if you should press her on what she just said. As you ponder, Horkenn pulls one chair while Orroah pulls another, and the two just stare at each other expectantly. After a moment, they both laugh and walk round the table to sit in the chair the other was offering for them. “Wouldn’t be able to laugh like that without it,” Horkenn said with a grim tone, taking a box out from under the table and opening it to reveal what appears to be a large thermos and a variety of cups of various sizes, shapes and colors. Again, you wonder if you should ask but feel as though you should let them go at their pace. Horkenn takes the thermos while Orroah pulls out two cups and puts them in front, before pushing the box towards you and inviting you to choose the one you prefer. You pick one shaped like one massive lily-of-the-valley almost at random and hold it while Horkenn pours you a cup of steaming hot tea. The wonderful fragrance hits you instantly, and you can swear you recognize some of the smells. A floral tea, using some of their own flowers? You take a careful sip and lean back, enjoying the peculiar but rather interesting taste of the tea while taking in the sight of the plants growing around you. You expect either of the shopkeepers to start expanding upon what they mentioned earlier, but neither of them speak of it yet. In fact, neither speak at all. They both sit there, sipping their tea and leaning back in their chairs, much like you. The three of you simply sit in silence, in this beautiful little piece of heaven cut away and hidden from the bustle and cold of the city, enjoying the calm and forgetting about everything else for a moment. You gaze around the flowers, at the fountain flowing out of a stack of black rocks, at your cup of tea, and at your hospitable hosts who do much the same, beatific smiles on their faces which only grow when they meet your eyes. ]
